universalActions.name: Attack
universalActions.description: Strike a target with an equipped weapon or unarmed. Roll discipline (arena) vs target Defense or Evasion. Weapon chassis determines effect tiers (damage track). Unarmed uses the Brawl chassis. Firing a ranged weapon while Engaged presents an Opening to all enemies in your Engaged Zone.
universalActions.risk: Firing a ranged weapon while Engaged presents an Opening to all enemies in your Engaged Zone.
universalActions.note: Load equipped weapons to see available attack options and applicable discipline gambits.

universalActions.name: Aim
universalActions.description: Line up a careful shot or study a target's defenses. Roll Ranged (Reflex). Power = Roll.
universalActions.risk: Requires focus — you must have line of sight to the target.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Gain [Optimized] on your next attack against the aimed target.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Gain [Optimized] on your next attack. If the attack hits, step up the Effect by 1 Tier.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Gain [Optimized] and [Empowered] on your next attack against the aimed target.

universalActions.name: Move
universalActions.description: Move up to 2 zones. Diceless unless you are Engaged — if Engaged, you must roll to disengage (presenting an Opening on failure). In vehicle encounters, uses Piloting. When rolled (Engaged), the result tier determines the quality of the disengage.
universalActions.risk: If Engaged, failing to disengage presents an Opening to the enemy.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Move up to 2 zones. No Opening presented.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Move up to 3 zones. No Opening presented.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Move up to 3 zones. No Opening presented. Gain [Optimized] on Dodge until end of round.

universalActions.name: Reload
universalActions.description: Reload a weapon that has expended its ammunition. Restores the weapon's clip to full capacity.

universalActions.name: Take Cover
universalActions.description: Move to the nearest cover in your zone. Gain [Cover 1] (or [Cover 2] for heavy fortifications, GM adjudicates). Requires cover to be available in the zone.

universalActions.name: Draw / Holster
universalActions.description: Draw a carried weapon to equipped status, or holster an equipped weapon. Switching weapons requires drawing the new one (one Maneuver per weapon).

universalActions.name: Assess
universalActions.description: Study a target or situation. Roll a relevant discipline (Investigation, Insight, Survival, etc. — GM picks what fits the approach). Power = Roll for environmental assessment; vs target's Resolve in social or opposed contexts. Ask the GM one question per tier of effect.
universalActions.risk: Requires focus — you may miss details if the situation shifts around you.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Ask the GM 1 question about the target or situation. The GM answers honestly based on what your character could reasonably observe.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Ask the GM 2 questions. Answers may include details not immediately obvious — tactical weaknesses, emotional states, hidden elements.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Ask the GM 3 questions. The GM provides detailed, actionable intelligence. You may learn things the target is actively trying to hide.

universalActions.name: Treat Injury
universalActions.description: Perform medical treatment on self or ally. Roll Medicine (Wits). Power = Roll, but each point of [Stimmed] on the patient steps down your Medicine Power die by one size (D12→D10→D8→D6→D4). Primarily used during Short and Long Rests to enhance [Natural Recovery] baselines. Consuming a Medpac adds +1 Tier (patient gains [Stimmed] +1).
universalActions.risk: Botching treatment may worsen the patient's condition. Treating in Combat presents an Opening — you are distracted and vulnerable.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Increase Trauma recovery by 1 for all allies resting with you. (Short rest: 2 Trauma instead of 1. Long rest: 3 instead of 2.)

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: As Fleeting, plus allies may convert 2 Vitality into 1 additional Trauma recovery. Short-term penalty until next rest.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Miracle Worker. Increase Vitality regeneration by 1 for all allies. (Short rest: recover 1 Vitality. Long rest: recover 2 Vitality.)

universalActions.painkillerProtocol: If you have capped out on the Treat Injury ladder and have tier remaining, spend 1 tier: patient gains [Buffered 3] going into the next scene. No additional [Stimmed] — same treatment, same operation.

universalActions.name: Interact
universalActions.description: Perform a non-combat task under pressure — operate a console, pick a lock, disarm a bomb, hotwire a speeder, or any physical challenge that demands skill and time. Roll a relevant discipline (Tech, Skulduggery, Survival, etc. — GM picks what fits the task). Power = Roll for uncontested tasks; vs difficulty set by GM for contested or complex challenges.
universalActions.risk: Fumbling under pressure may set off alarms, break the device, or waste precious time.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Partial progress or simple interaction. You make headway but the task is not complete — a second Interact may be needed. Simple tasks (flip a switch, open an unlocked door) resolve fully.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Complete a standard task or make significant progress on a complex challenge. Locks open, consoles respond, devices activate.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Complete a complex task in a single action, or finish a standard task with a bonus outcome — the sliced terminal also reveals useful intel, the disarmed bomb yields salvageable components, the hotwired speeder starts silently.

universalActions.name: Join Battle
universalActions.description: Roll initiative when combat begins. Roll Tactics (Wits). Your raw Power Die result is your place in the turn order — higher results act first. If a gambit or effect causes you to spend a tier of initiative, your position drops to the midpoint of the next band down (Fleeting → 2, Masterful → 6, Legendary → 10, Unleashed → 14).
universalActions.risk: Failed Control (1-3): You are surprised — you gain [Disoriented] and [Exposed] until the end of your first turn. You still act in your rolled order, but carry these conditions into your opening action.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Act at position 1-3 in initiative order.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Act at position 4-7 in initiative order.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Act at position 8-11 in initiative order.

effect.label: Unleashed
effect.description: Act at position 12-15 in initiative order.

universalActions.name: Dodge
universalActions.description: Reactive evasive defense. When targeted by a physical attack, roll Evasion (Reflex) to avoid or reduce the incoming damage. Free — does not cost your Exploit pip.
universalActions.risk: If you fail, the attack lands fully.

universalActions.name: Endure
universalActions.description: Reactive absorption defense. When targeted by a physical attack, roll Endure (Physique) to absorb or reduce the incoming damage. Free — does not cost your Exploit pip.
universalActions.risk: If you fail, the attack lands fully.

universalActions.name: Resist
universalActions.description: Reactive mental defense. When targeted by a mental or social attack (Intimidation, Force influence, fear effects), roll Resolve (Grit) to resist the effect. Free — does not cost your Exploit pip.
universalActions.risk: If you fail, the mental effect lands fully.

universalActions.name: Coordinate
universalActions.description: Direct NPC allies to act with purpose. Roll Tactics (Wits). Power = Roll.
universalActions.risk: NPCs may hesitate, misinterpret, or refuse suicidal orders.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: One NPC performs a basic action — hold position, fall back, suppressing fire. No creativity.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: NPC acts with initiative and timing. One ally gains [Optimized] on their next action.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Up to 3 NPC allies execute a synchronized action. All gain [Optimized].

universalActions.name: Command Beast
universalActions.description: Direct a beast companion to act. Roll Beast Handling (Grit). Power = Roll. The creature shares your action economy — commanding it costs your Maneuver, and if the creature Attacks, it costs your Action.
universalActions.risk: Untrained or spooked creatures may resist, bolt, or act on instinct.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Creature obeys a simple instruction — move, stay, guard. Positioning only, no attacks.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Creature acts with purpose. If directing it to Attack (costs your Action), the attack gains [Optimized].

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Perfect communion. Beast Attack gains [Optimized] + [Empowered (Grit)]. Creature is immune to fear until end of round.

forceManeuvers.name: Centering Focus
forceManeuvers.description: Channel the Force inward to regulate body and spirit. Roll Control (Grit). Power = Roll.
forceManeuvers.risk: Failure disrupts your inner calm — your focus fragments briefly.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Clear 1 Lingering condition from yourself.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Clear 1 Ongoing condition from yourself.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Ignore Trauma penalties in 1 arena until your next turn.

forceManeuvers.name: Force Sense
forceManeuvers.description: Expand awareness beyond physical senses. Roll Sense (Wits). Power = Roll.
forceManeuvers.risk: Overextending your senses may leave you momentarily vulnerable.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Ask the GM 1 question about the Force, the living world, or hidden presences. The Force whispers one truth.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Ask the GM 2 questions. The Force reveals connections, intentions, or dangers not visible to normal senses.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Ask the GM 3 questions. The Force opens fully — visions, echoes, and deep truths. The GM provides detailed, actionable intelligence.

forceManeuvers.name: Telekinesis
forceManeuvers.description: Project the Force outward to lift, move, or manipulate objects in the scene. Roll Alter (Presence). Power = Roll. This is pure object manipulation — moving things with your mind. For pushing/pulling people, see the Telekinetic Savant’s Kinetic Impulse.
forceManeuvers.risk: Losing focus may drop the object unexpectedly or move it to an unintended location.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Move a small object (blaster, datapad, chair, tool). Lift, slide, or reposition within the scene.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Move a medium object (crate, speeder door, furniture, body). Manipulate with enough force to barricade a door, clear debris, or rearrange a room.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Move a large object (vehicle, structural element, heavy machinery). Collapse a walkway, lift a speeder, or tear a blast door from its frame.

gambits.name: Breakneck Pace
gambits.rule: After resolving a Move, spend 1 tier. Move an additional 1 zone.

gambits.name: Bantha Rush
gambits.rule: After resolving a melee Attack, spend 1 tier. Push the target 1 zone away. You may follow them into that zone.

gambits.name: The Slam
gambits.rule: After resolving a melee Attack, spend 1 tier. Push the target 1 zone and follow. The target gains [Prone].

gambits.name: Clinch
gambits.rule: After resolving an unarmed Attack, spend 1 tier. Target gains [Slowed] Tactical.

gambits.name: Takedown
gambits.rule: After resolving an unarmed Attack, spend 1 tier. Target gains [Prone].

gambits.name: Vice Grip
gambits.rule: After resolving an unarmed Attack, spend 1 tier. Target gains [Restrained] Tactical. While restrained, your unarmed attacks against them gain [Empowered (Physique)].

gambits.name: Disarming Smile
gambits.rule: After resolving a social action [Narrative], spend 1 tier. Gain [Optimized] on all social actions against this target for the scene. Free if Hope is dominant on the Destiny tracker.

gambits.name: Silver Tongue
gambits.rule: After resolving a social action [Narrative], spend 1 tier. The target's Social Resistance drops by 2 for the scene. Only applies to non-threatening approaches (Charm, Persuasion).

gambits.name: Sway
gambits.rule: After resolving a social action [Narrative], spend 1 tier. Convince the target to do something they wouldn't normally do — share a secret, grant a favor, bend a rule. Must be plausible within the fiction; the GM adjudicates limits.

gambits.name: Feint
gambits.rule: After resolving a melee Attack, spend 1 tier. Target gains [Exposed] to your next attack. Tactical.

gambits.name: Misdirect
gambits.rule: After resolving a social action [Narrative], spend 1 tier. The target wastes their next significant action pursuing false information or a phantom threat you planted.

gambits.name: False Flag
gambits.rule: After resolving a social action [Narrative], spend 1 tier. Convince the target you represent a faction, authority, or allegiance you do not. Grants narrative permissions associated with that identity until exposed.

gambits.name: Brace
gambits.rule: After resolving an Endure defense, spend 1 tier. Gain [Buffered 1].

gambits.name: Shake It Off
gambits.rule: After resolving an Endure defense, spend 1 tier. Clear 1 Lingering physical condition.

gambits.name: Dig In
gambits.rule: After resolving an Endure defense, spend 1 tier. Ignore the penalties of all Trauma until the end of your next turn.

gambits.name: Sidestep
gambits.rule: After resolving a Dodge defense, spend 1 tier. Immediately move 1 zone. This does not cost a Maneuver.

gambits.name: Untouchable
gambits.rule: After resolving a Dodge defense, spend 1 tier. The attacker gains [Exposed] to your next attack. Tactical.

gambits.name: Ghost
gambits.rule: After resolving a Dodge defense, spend 1 tier. All enemies in the zone lose track of you. They must succeed on an Assess before they can target you again.

gambits.name: Walking Fire
gambits.rule: After resolving a heavy weapons Attack, spend 1 tier. Target gains [Suppressed] Tactical.

gambits.name: Danger Close
gambits.rule: After resolving a heavy weapons Attack, spend 1 tier. Apply the attack to all targets in the zone. Compare Power die to each target's Defense separately. Step down the tier effect on each by one.

gambits.name: Scorched Earth
gambits.rule: After resolving a heavy weapons Attack, spend 1 tier. The target zone gains [Hazard 2] Ongoing. Anyone entering or starting their turn in the zone takes the Hazard damage. Source: fire/explosion. Recovery: leave the zone.

gambits.name: Read Between the Lines
gambits.rule: After resolving a social action [Narrative], spend 1 tier. The GM reveals one thing the target is hiding, avoiding, or lying about in this conversation.

gambits.name: Weak Point
gambits.rule: After resolving an Assess, spend 1 tier. Your next attack against this target ignores their [Guarded] or [Cover] rating.

gambits.name: Pattern Lock
gambits.rule: After resolving an Assess, spend 1 tier. The target gains [Exposed] to your attacks. Tactical.

gambits.name: Menacing Strike
gambits.rule: After resolving a melee Attack [Melee, Unarmed], spend 1 tier. Target gains [Rattled] Tactical. Free if Toll is dominant on the Destiny tracker.

gambits.name: Break Composure
gambits.rule: After resolving a social action [Narrative], spend 1 tier. The target reveals their primary motivation in this scene — what they want, protect, or fear. The GM provides a concrete, actionable detail.

gambits.name: Demoralizing Strike
gambits.rule: After resolving an Attack [Any], spend 1 tier. Target's Resolve defense reduced by 2. Ongoing.

gambits.name: Tactical Scan
gambits.rule: After resolving an Assess, spend 1 tier. Gain [Optimized] on defense rolls against one target in your zone. Tactical.

gambits.name: Battlefield Read
gambits.rule: After resolving an Assess, spend 1 tier. Gain [Optimized] on defense rolls against all targets in your zone. Tactical.

gambits.name: Instant Reconstruction
gambits.rule: After resolving an Assess [Narrative], spend 1 tier. This also costs your Action pip. You instantly read the entire scene — what would normally take minutes, you process in a single action. Your Assess questions require no time if the information is available.

gambits.name: Field Treatment
gambits.rule: After resolving a Treat Injury, spend 1 tier. Remove one additional condition treatable by medicine from one patient.

gambits.name: Trauma Surgery
gambits.rule: After resolving a Treat Injury, spend 1 tier. Remove 1 additional Trauma from one patient.

gambits.name: Miracle Worker
gambits.rule: After resolving a Treat Injury, spend 1 tier. Restore 1 additional Vitality to one patient.

gambits.name: Parry
gambits.rule: After resolving a melee Attack [Melee], spend 1 tier. Gain [Optimized] on all Endure defense rolls. Tactical.

gambits.name: Exposing Strike
gambits.rule: After resolving a melee Attack [Melee, Unarmed], spend 1 tier. The target immediately presents an Opening — you or any ally in the zone may spend an Exploit pip to react.

gambits.name: Cleaving Arc
gambits.rule: After resolving a melee Attack [Melee, Unarmed], spend 1 tier. Apply your attack to a second target in the same zone. If the second target has higher Presence than the original, it automatically fails. Compare Power die to each target's Defense separately, then step down the tier effect on each by one.

gambits.name: Common Ground
gambits.rule: After resolving a social action [Narrative], spend 1 tier. The GM reveals what the target values or wants — something you can offer or leverage. Free if Hope is dominant on the Destiny tracker.

gambits.name: The Deal
gambits.rule: After resolving a social action [Narrative], spend 1 tier. The target's Social Resistance drops by 2 for the scene. Only applies to non-threatening approaches (Charm, Persuasion).

gambits.name: Turncoat
gambits.rule: After resolving a social action [Narrative], spend 1 tier. Convince the target to switch sides, betray an ally, or act against their current allegiance — provided you've offered something they want more. The GM adjudicates sufficiency.

gambits.name: Evasive Maneuver
gambits.rule: After resolving a Move [Vehicle], spend 1 tier. Your vehicle is treated as 1 scale smaller for defensive actions. Tactical.

gambits.name: Attack Vector
gambits.rule: After resolving a Move [Vehicle], spend 1 tier. One gunner on your vehicle gains [Optimized] on their next attack.

gambits.name: Bold Maneuver
gambits.rule: After resolving a Move [Vehicle], spend 1 tier. The next attack from your vehicle is treated as 1 scale larger.

gambits.name: Suppressive Fire
gambits.rule: After resolving a ranged Attack [Ranged], spend 1 tier. Target gains [Rattled] Tactical.

gambits.name: Trick Shot
gambits.rule: After resolving a ranged Attack [Ranged], spend 1 tier. Shoot an environmental element — a door release, light fixture, cable, fuel line — triggering a narrative effect in the zone. GM adjudicates.

gambits.name: Pinning Shot
gambits.rule: After resolving a ranged Attack [Ranged], spend 1 tier. Target gains [Suppressed] Tactical.

gambits.name: Iron Will
gambits.rule: After resolving a defense against a social, mental, or Force-based attack, spend 1 tier. Clear 1 mental condition ([Rattled] or [Shaken]) from yourself.

gambits.name: Unshakeable
gambits.rule: After resolving a defense against a social, mental, or Force-based attack, spend 1 tier. You are immune to [Rattled] and [Shaken] until the end of your next turn.

gambits.name: Rebuke
gambits.rule: After resolving a defense against a social, mental, or Force-based attack, spend 1 tier. The attacker gains [Rattled] Tactical. Your defiance breaks their momentum — their next action against you is [Weakened (Presence)].

gambits.name: Danger Sense
gambits.rule: After resolving an Assess, spend 1 tier. You cannot be surprised until the end of the scene. Ambushes against you automatically fail — you act in the first round regardless of Stealth results. If your Join Battle roll results in Failed Control, you ignore the surprised state and do not gain [Disoriented] or [Exposed].

gambits.name: Read Intent
gambits.rule: After resolving an Assess, spend 1 tier. The GM reveals the target's next intended action — what they plan to do on their next turn. If the target changes their action after this reveal, they suffer [Disoriented] Immediate from the cognitive disruption of being read.

gambits.name: Precognition
gambits.rule: After resolving an Assess, spend 1 tier. The Force shows you the shape of incoming violence before it arrives. Gain [Optimized] on Endure and Dodge defense rolls. Tactical.

gambits.name: Sleight of Hand
gambits.rule: After resolving an Interact, spend 1 tier. You plant or lift one small item (tracking device, data chip, holdout blaster, keycard) without the target noticing in the same action. The GM adjudicates what counts as 'small.'

gambits.name: Bypass
gambits.rule: After resolving an Interact with a lock, trap, or security device, spend 1 tier. You disable it without leaving any evidence of tampering. No alarm, no trace, no sign the device was ever touched.

gambits.name: Holdout
gambits.rule: After resolving an Attack, spend 1 tier. You draw and fire a concealed hold-out weapon in one fluid motion — the weapon was hidden and the attack came without warning. The target gains [Disoriented] Tactical.

gambits.name: Leave No Trace
gambits.rule: After resolving a Move, spend 1 tier. You leave no physical evidence of your passage — no footprints, no displaced objects, no scent trail. Attempts to track you through this area automatically fail unless the tracker has supernatural means.

gambits.name: Lying Low
gambits.rule: After resolving Join Battle, spend 1 tier of initiative (your position drops to the midpoint of the next band down). You blend into the chaos of the opening moment. You cannot be targeted by any enemy during round 1 of combat.

gambits.name: Vanish
gambits.rule: After resolving a Move, spend 1 tier. You disappear from the scene while attention is elsewhere. You are simply gone — no one saw you leave, and no one can determine where you went without supernatural means or dedicated investigation.

gambits.name: Forage
gambits.rule: After resolving an Interact in a natural or settled environment, spend 1 tier. You find enough food, water, and basic supplies to sustain up to 5 individuals for one Long Rest. The GM adjudicates availability based on the environment.

gambits.name: Natural Remedy
gambits.rule: After resolving a Treat Injury during a Short Rest, spend 1 tier. One patient avoids gaining [Stimmed] from this treatment. Requires access to a natural environment — herbs, clean water, or organic compounds replace synthetic medicine.

gambits.name: Pathfinder
gambits.rule: After resolving an Assess, spend 1 tier. You chart a highly actionable route through the terrain. The GM reveals one complication or opportunity along the path, and all actions taken to navigate or avoid hazards on that route gain [Optimized].

gambits.name: Coordinated Fire
gambits.rule: After resolving an Aim, spend 1 tier. Share your Aim benefit at the reduced tier with one ally who can hear you. That ally gains [Optimized] on their next attack against the same target.

gambits.name: Tactical Reposition
gambits.rule: After resolving a Move, spend 1 tier. One ally who can hear you may immediately perform the same Move effect at your reduced tier as a free reaction. Their movement does not provoke Openings.

gambits.name: Covering Fire
gambits.rule: After resolving a ranged Attack, spend 1 tier. Choose one ally. The target cannot Exploit Openings against that ally. Tactical. This effect breaks immediately if the chosen ally engages the target in melee or attacks the target directly.

gambits.name: Overclock
gambits.rule: After resolving an Interact with a device, spend 1 tier. The device's actions gain [Empowered] for the rest of the scene. However, any natural 1 rolled on the Power die while the device is overclocked immediately disables the device — it shuts down and requires repair.

gambits.name: Splice
gambits.rule: After resolving an Interact with a networked system, spend 1 tier. Gain a Fleeting Assess — ask the GM one question about data, surveillance feeds, communications, or system status connected to the device you spliced.

gambits.name: Astrogation
gambits.rule: After resolving an Assess, spend 1 tier. You calculate a hyperspace route or complex navigation solution. All navigation actions on this route gain [Optimized]. The GM reveals one complication or opportunity along the calculated path.

gambits.name: Force Absorb
gambits.rule: After resolving an Endure defense, spend 1 tier. Gain [Buffered 1]. The Force cushions the impact — energy disperses through your body rather than breaking it.

gambits.name: Healing Trance
gambits.rule: After resolving a Treat Injury on yourself, spend 1 tier. Clear 1 additional Trauma without consuming a Medpac. No [Stimmed] increase. This counts as rest-assisted treatment — cannot be used in combat.

gambits.name: Beyond Pain
gambits.rule: After resolving an Endure defense, spend 1 tier. Ignore all Trauma penalties and all active conditions until the end of your next turn. Your body is a vessel of the Force — pain is irrelevant. When this effect ends, all suppressed conditions resume.

gambits.name: Force Shove
gambits.rule: After resolving an Attack, spend 1 tier. Push the target 1 zone away. This is a telekinetic shove — no physical contact required. Range: your zone or adjacent zone.

gambits.name: Mind Trick
gambits.rule: After resolving a social action [Narrative], spend 1 tier. Implant a single short suggestion in a weak-willed target's mind. The suggestion must be simple and not directly self-destructive ('You don't need to see our identification,' 'You should go check the east corridor'). Strong-willed targets or those trained to resist (Resolve D10+) are immune.

gambits.name: Hurl
gambits.rule: After rolling Telekinesis, spend 1 tier. The object you lifted becomes a projectile — you channel your telekinetic energy into the throw. This grants permission to throw the object at a single target on your next Attack action using Alter (Presence). Damage = 1 per tier achieved on the Attack roll + 1 per tier remaining from the Telekinesis roll after spending. The object’s scale drops by one tier (you redirected Force energy from lifting to throwing).

gambits.name: Read the Beast
gambits.rule: After resolving an Assess, spend 1 tier. The GM reveals the beast's temperament and one behavioral trigger — what calms it, what enrages it, or what it fears. Does not countermand an active command on trained beasts.

gambits.name: Steady Nerve
gambits.rule: After resolving an Interact, spend 1 tier. Suppress a beast's hostility or panic. Tactical. The beast will not attack or flee while the effect lasts. Does not work on beasts currently in combat or trained beasts following an active command. Does not countermand an active command on trained beasts.

gambits.name: Beast Whisperer
gambits.rule: After resolving an Interact, spend 1 tier. Establish temporary rapport with a beast. You may make one significant request — guard a location, carry a rider, lead you to water, or track a scent. The beast cooperates willingly for the scene. Does not countermand an active command on trained beasts.

vocationManeuvers.name: Dead Drop
vocationManeuvers.description: Draw down on a target and force them to react — you don't fire, you just let them see where this is going. Roll Ranged (Reflex) vs target's Resolve.
vocationManeuvers.risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target — you've shown your hand.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled].

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled]. Choose one: the target must spend their next Maneuver taking Cover, OR they present an Opening to you.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled] and [Exposed]. They present an Opening to you. If you Exploit it this round, step up Effect by 1 Tier.

vocationManeuvers.name: Slip
vocationManeuvers.description: Melt out of a target's awareness mid-fight. Roll Stealth (Reflex) vs target's Resolve. You don't move — you just stop being seen.
vocationManeuvers.risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target — they see right through it.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Gain Concealment against the target until your next action. Their attacks against you lose [Optimized].

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Gain Concealment against the target until end of round. They cannot target you with abilities that require line of sight.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Gain Concealment against all enemies in your zone until end of round. You may immediately Move 1 zone without presenting an Opening.

vocationManeuvers.name: Arise
vocationManeuvers.description: Channel ichor into fallen creatures or corpses in your zone, animating them as thralls. Roll Alter (Presence). Corpses must be physically present in the fiction. Directing Thralls: Use the Coordinate maneuver, substituting Control (Grit) for Tactics (Wits). If you are [Incapacitated], all thralls collapse.
vocationManeuvers.risk: On failure, the ichor dissipates — the dead do not answer. Wasted Action.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Animate 1 corpse as a simple thrall. Acts on your initiative. Collapses at end of round.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Animate up to 2 corpses as thralls. Act on your initiative, last until end of scene.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Animate up to 3 corpses as thralls. Act on your initiative, last until end of scene. GM may grant them one echo ability from their living stat block.

vocationManeuvers.name: True Possession
vocationManeuvers.description: Fully project your spirit into the target's body. Roll Alter (Presence) vs target's Resolve. Your own body goes [Helpless] for the duration. Line of sight required (unless using Pervert the Bond through an Ichor Talisman). Sustain: 1 Edge Point per round to maintain. If you can't pay, possession ends immediately — you snap back to your body [Disoriented].
vocationManeuvers.risk: On failure, the target's mind lashes back — you gain [Rattled] + [Disoriented] until end of round. The target knows someone tried to get inside their head.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: The host spirit fights hard. All actions through the possessed body suffer [Disoriented] + [Weakened]. Sustaining also costs your Maneuver each round in addition to 1 Edge.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: The spirit resists but you have the upper hand. Actions through the possessed body suffer [Disoriented] only. Costs 1 Edge per round to sustain.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Total override. Full actions, no penalties. The host spirit is suppressed entirely. Still costs 1 Edge per round. Target retains no memory of what happened during possession.

vocationManeuvers.name: Read the Room
vocationManeuvers.description: Study a target's body language, tone, and social cues. Roll Charm (Presence) — no defense, this is an Assess-style read. You learn what makes them tick and how to use it.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Learn one useful detail about the target's emotional state, motivation, or social leverage.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Learn the above, plus choose one: gain +1 tier on your next social action against this target, OR your next social action against them is [Optimized].

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Learn the above. You gain both benefits — +1 tier AND [Optimized] on your next social action against the target.

vocationManeuvers.name: Compel
vocationManeuvers.description: Make a demand, issue a directive, or simply make it clear what you expect. Roll Charm (Presence) vs target's Resolve. Pure social pressure — no threats, no lies, just the weight of your personality.
vocationManeuvers.risk: On failure, the target sees through the play — they are immune to Compel from you for the rest of the scene, and the GM may shift their attitude one step hostile.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target's next interaction with you or your crew is [Disoriented] — they're off-balance, second-guessing.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target must either comply with a reasonable request OR publicly justify their refusal. GM reveals one thing the target is trying to protect or hide.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target complies with your request. Their attitude toward you shifts one step favorable for the rest of the scene.

vocationManeuvers.name: Keen Insight
vocationManeuvers.description: Dedicate your full attention to a single person, object, or location that the GM confirms is narratively significant — a crime scene, a key NPC, a suspicious artifact, a location tied to an active lead. If the target holds no relevant information, the GM may simply say "Nothing to see here" and the maneuver is not spent. This goes deeper than Assess — you're not asking questions, you're reconstructing what happened.
vocationManeuvers.risk: Tunnel vision. You are fully absorbed — you cannot act on or respond to anything else happening in the scene until your next beat. If the situation shifts around you, you miss it entirely.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Learn the most recent significant event involving the subject — who was here, what they did, approximately when.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Learn the above, plus the GM reveals a hidden detail — something concealed, altered, or deliberately obscured. A false trail, a removed object, a covered wound.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Full reconstruction. The GM narrates a brief flashback of what happened at this location or to this subject. You see it play out. The crew may act on this as reliable intelligence.

vocationManeuvers.name: Shatterpoint Sense
vocationManeuvers.description: Perceive the connections between people, events, and objects through the Force. Roll Sense (Wits), no defense. You do not learn facts — you perceive relationships, emotional weight, and the web of causality that binds things together.
vocationManeuvers.risk: On failure, you gain [Disoriented]. Particularly traumatic echoes may add [Rattled] at the GM’s discretion.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: A vague sense that a connection exists between two elements in the scene. You know something links them, but not the nature or significance.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: You perceive the nature and emotional weight of the connection. The GM reveals the relationship’s character — loyalty, fear, debt, hatred, love, obligation. You may ask 1 question about the connection.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: The full web of connections unfolds. You see how multiple elements relate — the chain of causality, the pressure points, the fault lines. The GM reveals the key pressure point in the web. You may ask 2 questions.

vocationManeuvers.name: Shatterpoint Strike
vocationManeuvers.description: Once per scene. Roll Sense (Wits) vs target’s Resolve. You perceive the shatterpoint — the exact fracture line in the target’s defense, stance, or resolve. You must attack the target before the end of the round or the moment passes.
vocationManeuvers.risk: On failure, the maneuver is wasted and the target knows you tried to read them — they sense the probe.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target gains [Exposed].

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target gains [Exposed] and [Optimized] applies to your next attack against them this round.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target gains [Exposed]. Your next attack against them this round gains [Optimized] and [Empowered].

vocationManeuvers.name: Enhance Attribute
vocationManeuvers.description: Diceless Maneuver. Spend 1 Edge + your Maneuver pip. Choose one arena: Physique or Reflex. That arena gains +1 Effect Tier on all rolls. Duration: Tactical. At the start of each subsequent turn, you may spend 1 Edge to sustain the enhancement — no action cost. You may only enhance one arena at a time. Switching arenas requires a new activation.

vocationManeuvers.name: Force Speed
vocationManeuvers.description: Channel the Force into explosive velocity. Roll Control (Grit). The result generates Move Points that must be spent on your Move action this turn (1 point = 1 zone). Movement fueled by Force Speed presents NO Openings, even when leaving Engaged zones. Unspent points are lost at the end of your turn. Force Speed movement fuels Art of Movement traversal (wall-running, vertical movement).
vocationManeuvers.risk: Failure wastes the maneuver — the Force does not answer your call for speed this round.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: 2 Move Points.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: 4 Move Points.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: 6 Move Points.

vocationManeuvers.name: Emptiness
vocationManeuvers.description: Short Rest only. Roll Control (Grit). The current Toll count on the Destiny tracker sets the static difficulty. While meditating (narrative truth, always active during the rest): you are undetectable by Force senses, you clear 1 mental condition ([Rattled] or [Disoriented]), and you are physically vulnerable and interruptible. To carry effects into the next scene, spend 1 Edge.
vocationManeuvers.risk: On failure, the Edge spent to carry effects forward is wasted — the meditation did not reach sufficient depth.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Force detection against you is [Weakened] until the end of the next scene.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: As Fleeting. Additionally, you are immune to [Rattled] and [Disoriented] until the end of the next scene.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: As Masterful. Additionally, gain +1 Effect Tier on all Control (Grit) rolls until the end of the next scene.

vocationManeuvers.name: Kinetic Impulse
vocationManeuvers.description: Project the Force outward to push or pull a target through space. Roll Alter (Presence) vs target’s Defense. Choose push or pull direction on hit. This is weaponized telekinesis — directing the Force at people, not just objects.
vocationManeuvers.risk: Losing control of the Force may cause unintended collateral damage.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Push or pull the target 1 zone in any direction.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Push or pull the target 2 zones. Target gains [Disoriented] Tactical.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Push or pull the target 3 zones. Target gains [Prone]. If the target collides with a solid boundary before completing their forced movement, they take 1 Vitality damage per zone of movement not completed.

vocationManeuvers.name: Kinetic Combat
vocationManeuvers.description: Telekinetically wield a weapon — it fights independently, guided by your will. Roll Alter (Presence) to activate. The weapon can target enemies in your zone or an adjacent zone. Attacks made through Kinetic Combat use Melee (Presence) — attribute swap. Sustained: spend your Maneuver each round to maintain (no additional roll). To re-roll for a better tier, you may roll again instead of sustaining — failure means the weapon drops wherever it was. If you do not spend your Maneuver to sustain, the weapon falls.
vocationManeuvers.risk: Failure on activation or re-establishment means the weapon drops — you lose telekinetic control.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: The weapon can Attack as your Action using Melee (Presence). Raw strikes only — no Gambits, no Exploits. Your control is sufficient to fight, but not to finesse.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: As Fleeting, but your form is precise enough to use any qualifying Gambits on the attack. GM discretion on edge cases requiring physical contact.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: As Masterful, plus the weapon can use Exploits. Full combat capability through pure telekinetic mastery.
